metadata.success-criteria: ALL of these must pass or the system is BROKEN: 1. pulumi destroy + pulumi up creates all GCP infra from scratch 2. Docker image builds and pushes to GAR 3. DVC data pulls inside SkyPilot setup 4. 4 models train on GCP L4 spot with finite val_loss in MLflow 5. Artifacts (checkpoints, configs) uploaded to GCS via multipart 6. mlruns sync back to local machine works 7. RunPod dev workflow: launch → train → mlruns on network volume → sync back If ANY component fails → SYSTEM FAILURE → fix with ralph-loop before proceeding

task.name: Destroy GCP Pulumi stack

commands.cmd: # Verify: 0 instances, 0 Cloud Run services, 0 GCS buckets

task.acceptance: pulumi stack shows no resources. GCP console is empty.

task.name: Deploy GCP Pulumi stack
task.description: Fresh pulumi up: Cloud SQL (PostgreSQL), 3 GCS buckets, GAR registry, Cloud Run MLflow 3.10.0, IAM service accounts, Secret Manager (SA key). This tests: can a new user provision all GCP infra with one command?
task.acceptance: All resources created. pulumi stack output shows: - mlflow_url (Cloud Run URL) - cloud_sql_ip - gcs_bucket names - gar_repository URL

task.name: Update .env with fresh Pulumi outputs
task.description: Write MLFLOW_GCP_URI and other Pulumi outputs to .env. Rule #22: .env.example is single source of truth.
task.acceptance: .env has MLFLOW_GCP_URI from pulumi stack output.

task.name: Build Docker base image
task.acceptance: docker images minivess-base:latest shows fresh image.

task.name: Push to GAR

commands.cmd: docker tag minivess-base:latest $(pulumi stack output gar_image_url):latest
gcloud artifacts docker images list $(pulumi stack output gar_repository)

task.acceptance: GAR shows image with fresh CREATE_TIME.

commands.cmd: docker run --rm minivess-base:latest python -c " from minivess.config.models import TrainingConfig c = TrainingConfig() print(f'mixed_precision_val={c.mixed_precision_val}') from minivess.adapters.sam3_backbone import Sam3Backbone print('SAM3 backbone importable') from minivess.orchestration.flows.train_flow import training_flow print('Training flow importable') "

phase.name: GCP 4-Model Training Verification
phase.description:
Sequential launch of all 4 models on GCP L4 spot. Each model: SkyPilot provisions VM → pulls Docker from GAR → DVC pull in setup → trains 2 epochs → uploads metrics+artifacts to MLflow Cloud Run → auto-destroys. GPU quota = 1, so sequential only.
PASS CRITERIA PER MODEL: - MLflow experiment created with FINISHED status - val_loss is FINITE (not NaN, not Inf) - At least 1 checkpoint artifact uploaded to GCS - Training completes without OOM or crash

task.name: Train sam3_vanilla on GCP L4

commands.cmd: # Verify in MLflow: FINISHED + finite val_loss

task.ralph-loop: Monitor via ralph-loop. On failure: diagnose + fix + retry.

task.name: Train sam3_hybrid on GCP L4
task.description: CRITICAL: Must use smoke_sam3_hybrid_cloud.yaml (val_interval=1). The GCP YAML auto-selects _cloud config when available. If val_loss=NaN → check which config was used (sentinel bug).

task.name: Train vesselfm on GCP L4
task.description: VesselFM requires patch Z≥32 (5 stride-2 downsamplings). Docker image has the fix. Previous failure was SSH error (transient).

task.name: Verify ALL models in MLflow
task.description: Query MLflow Cloud Run for all 4 experiments. Every model must have FINISHED status with finite val_loss. If ANY model has NaN or FAILED, the SYSTEM IS BROKEN — do not proceed.
task.acceptance: 4/4 models: FINISHED with finite val_loss. (sam3_topolora excluded — needs A100, hardware limitation not system bug)

task.name: Verify GCS artifacts

commands.cmd: # Verify: checkpoint .pth files, config artifacts, metric history

task.acceptance: Artifacts exist in GCS for all 4 experiments.

task.name: Sync mlruns to local
task.description: Test the researcher workflow: cloud training done → pull results local. Use gsutil rsync to pull MLflow artifacts from GCS.
task.acceptance: Local mlruns/cloud/ has experiment data from all 4 models.

phase.name: RunPod Dev Workflow (Secondary)
phase.description: RunPod = dev environment for academic paper feature. SECONDARY priority. Tests: SkyPilot launch → Network Volume → DVC pull → train → filesystem mlruns → sync mlruns back to local.

task.name: Launch RunPod dev instance with Network Volume

commands.cmd: # SkyPilot launches RTX 4090 pod with /opt/vol network volume

task.acceptance: Cluster UP, DVC data pulled, sam3_vanilla trains.

task.name: Train sam3_vanilla on RunPod
task.acceptance: Training completes, mlruns/ on network volume has experiment.

phase.name: Integration Test Suite Hardening
phase.description: Write integration tests that catch system regressions. These run in CI-like mode (cloud credentials required) and verify the FULL pipeline works.

task.name: Write E2E system verification tests
task.description: TDD tests that verify the complete chain works: - Pulumi stack is up (Cloud Run responds) - GAR image exists and is recent - DVC data is accessible - SkyPilot can provision GCP instances - MLflow accepts artifact uploads These are NOT unit tests — they require real cloud credentials.

tdd-spec.red: test_mlflow_cloud_run_health: curl MLflow URL → 200 test_gar_image_exists: gcloud artifacts list → image present test_dvc_remote_accessible: dvc status -r upcloud → up to date test_gcs_buckets_exist: gsutil ls → 3 buckets test_skypilot_gcp_enabled: sky check gcp → enabled

task.name: Update CLAUDE.md with environment priority
task.description: Blast the env priority everywhere so future sessions don't ask which env: - Root CLAUDE.md: add "Environment Priority" section - deployment/CLAUDE.md: reinforce - .claude/metalearning/: add feedback doc

task.name: Write system verification report
task.description: Document: every component tested, every model trained, every artifact synced. Include MLflow experiment IDs, GCS bucket contents, timing, costs. This is the proof that the system works end-to-end.

cost-summary.item: Pulumi GCP stack (Cloud SQL + Cloud Run)
cost-summary.total: ~$5-10 total (dominated by GCP daily infra cost)

execution-notes.note: GCP prod/staging is PRIMARY. RunPod dev is SECONDARY.
DESTROY EVERYTHING first — test as a new user would.
If ANY phase fails → SYSTEM FAILURE → fix before proceeding.
ralph-loop monitors all cloud phases autonomously.
Sequential model launches on GCP (quota = 1 GPU).
sam3_topolora excluded (needs A100 — hardware limitation, not system bug).
